such a sign), the birth is said to take place in the 10th month;
if it is a fixed one, in the 11th month; and if a dual one, in the
12th month. The rule relating to Adhana can safely be applied
to queries regarding the health and sex of the child in the womb.
If there is a query as to when a particular woman who is preg-
nant will be delivered of the child, find out the stronger of the
two viz. the Ascendant and the Moon. Note the Dvadasamsa
of the stronger one. Count as many Rasis from this Dvadasamia-
Rasi as there are Dvadasamsas. When the Moon passes through
that sign the delivery will take place. For example, let us take
Aries as the Ascendant which is stronger than the Moon. Let
the 1/12 Arh^a of the Lagna be Capricorn which is the 10th

120 FUNDAMENTALS OF ASTROLOGY
Arh&a. So count ten signs from Capricorn and you come to
Libra. So the birth is likely to take place when the Moon passes
through Libra. There is another method of finding out the same
event.
